title: cyathea squarrosa
intro: Cyathea squarrosa, also known as the Rough Tree Fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical rainforests of Australia and New Guinea. The medicinal properties of Cyathea squarrosa are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe skin irritations. Cyathea squarrosa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
